description_long: This object is a lever-action rifle with a classic design, featuring a long, blued steel barrel and a wooden stock. The stock is a traditional fixed type, made from dark-stained wood, providing a sturdy shoulder rest. The receiver and lever mechanism are metallic, likely steel, and the lever loop is prominent beneath the action. The rifle is equipped with iron sights, with a rear notch and a front post visible along the barrel. The magazine is tubular, running parallel beneath the barrel, a hallmark of many lever-action rifles. The overall finish is smooth and matte, with the wood and metal components contrasting in color and texture.
description: A classic lever-action rifle with a long blued barrel, wooden fixed stock, and tubular magazine. Features iron sights and a smooth, matte finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the rifle, including the wooden stock, lever, and long barrel.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the narrow muzzle and alignment of the barrel, with the tubular magazine visible beneath.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the lever mechanism, wooden stock, and the length of the barrel.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the butt of the wooden stock and the alignment of the barrel and magazine tube.
